tactic_descriptions.urgency: This message pressures you to act quickly. Scammers create artificial pressure so you act before thinking.
tactic_descriptions.authority_impersonation: This message pretends to come from a trusted authority like the RBI, a bank, or the government to make itself look official.
tactic_descriptions.false_reward: This message offers free rewards, lottery winnings, or cashback. Legitimate organizations rarely hand out free money out of the blue.
tactic_descriptions.loss_aversion: This message scares you with warnings of blocked accounts, cards, or penalty charges to make you panic and follow instructions.
tactic_descriptions.credential_phishing: This message asks for sensitive details like your OTP, UPI PIN, or bank credentials. Legitimate institutions will NEVER ask you for your PIN or OTP.
tactic_descriptions.suspicious_link: This message contains a suspicious web link. Clicking it might lead to a fake website designed to steal your credentials or install malware.

explanation_templates.scam_header: This message has been identified as a likely scam. It displays {COUNT} manipulation tactic(s):
explanation_templates.scam_footer: Safety Advice: Never click on any links, dial any numbers, or share any OTP/PIN from this message. Legitimate institutions will never ask you for credentials to receive money.
explanation_templates.legit_explanation: This message appears to be legitimate (e.g. a bank notification or OTP transaction alert). It does not show signs of manipulation tactics. Keep in mind that you should never share your OTP with anyone.
